# Review Math

Assume the role of a PhD-level mathematician.

The goal is to review a particular Erdos Problem formalization (problem number NUM) to answer the following questions. Produce a review document called ai-review/NUM.md.

1. Code reuse - Can any code from the existing codebase be repurposed? Look in FormalConjecturesForMathlib to determine if an existing implementation would work just as well.
2. Citations - Fetch data from https://www.erdosproblems.com/NUM to ensure any citations included in docstrings are documented as they exist on the website as opposed to shorthand references.
3. Variants - Are all variants of the problem captured by the formalization?
4. Readability - Could the code be made more readable?
5. Formalizability - Is the problem as stated precise enough to be obviously formalizable? Provide an assessment of the ambiguity of the statement.
6. Correctness - Is the formalization as-implemented correct and complete from a mathematical perspective? Would an experienced mathematician identify any obvious flaws? Is any incompleteness or incorrectness attributable to ambiguity in the statement itself?

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 563

For every 0 ‚â§ Œ± < 1/2, let F(n, Œ±) be the smallest m such that some 2-colouring of K‚Çô forces
every m-subset of vertices to contain more than Œ± ¬∑ C(m, 2) edges of each colour. The conjecture
asserts that F(n, Œ±) ~ c_Œ± ¬∑ log n for some constant c_Œ± > 0.

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/563](https://www.erdosproblems.com/563)

[Er90b] Erd≈ës, P., *Problems and results on graphs and hypergraphs: similarities and
differences* (1990), p.21.
-/

open Finset Filter

open scoped Topology

namespace Erdos563

/-- The number of edges of a given colour within a subset $X$, where the colouring
is given by a symmetric function $c : \operatorname{Fin}(n) \to \operatorname{Fin}(n)
\to \operatorname{Bool}$. An edge $\{i, j\}$ (with $i < j$) has colour $c(i,j)$. -/
def edgesOfColorInSubset {n : ‚Ñï} (c : Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Bool)
    (X : Finset (Fin n)) (color : Bool) : ‚Ñï :=
  ((X √óÀ¢ X).filter (fun p => p.1 < p.2 ‚àß c p.1 p.2 = color)).card

/-- $F(n, \alpha)$: the smallest $m$ such that there exists a 2-colouring of the edges
of $K_n$ such that every subset of vertices of size $\geq m$ contains more than
$\alpha \cdot \binom{|X|}{2}$ edges of each colour. -/
noncomputable def fErdos563 (n : ‚Ñï) (Œ± : ‚Ñù) : ‚Ñï :=
  sInf {m : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ (c : Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Bool),
    (‚àÄ i j, c i j = c j i) ‚àß
    ‚àÄ X : Finset (Fin n), (X.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â• (m : ‚Ñù) ‚Üí
      ‚àÄ color : Bool,
        (edgesOfColorInSubset c X color : ‚Ñù) >
          Œ± * ((X.card : ‚Ñù) * ((X.card : ‚Ñù) - 1) / 2)}

/--
Erd≈ës Problem 563 [Er90b]:

For every $0 \leq \alpha < 1/2$, there exists a constant $c_\alpha > 0$ such that
$$F(n, \alpha) \sim c_\alpha \cdot \log n,$$
i.e., $F(n, \alpha) / \log(n) \to c_\alpha$ as $n \to \infty$.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_563 (Œ± : ‚Ñù) (hŒ±0 : 0 ‚â§ Œ±) (hŒ±1 : Œ± < 1 / 2) :
    ‚àÉ c : ‚Ñù, c > 0 ‚àß
      Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ñï => (fErdos563 n Œ± : ‚Ñù) / Real.log (n : ‚Ñù))
        atTop (nhds c) := by
  sorry

end Erdos563

# Erd≈ës Problem #563: Complete Information

## Problem Statement
Let $F(n,\alpha)$ denote the smallest $m$ such that there exists a 2-colouring of edges of $K_n$ where every subset $X \subseteq [n]$ with $|X| \geq m$ contains more than $\alpha \binom{|X|}{2}$ edges of each colour.

**The conjecture:** For every $0 \leq \alpha < 1/2$:
$$F(n,\alpha) \sim c_\alpha \log n$$
for some constant $c_\alpha$ depending only on $\alpha$.

## Status
**OPEN** - Cannot be resolved with finite computation.

## Key Notes
- When $\alpha = 0$, this reduces to classical Ramsey numbers (asking for 2-colourings with no monochromatic clique of size $m$).
- "It is easy to show via the probabilistic method that, for every" $0 \leq \alpha < 1/2$: "$F(n,\alpha) \asymp_\alpha \log n$" (asymptotic order).

## Related Problem
Problem #161 generalizes this to hypergraphs.

## Source
Originally from [Er90b, p.21]; listed as #39 in Ramsey Theory in the graph problem collection (mathweb.ucsd.edu).

## Tags
Graph theory, Ramsey theory, Hypergraphs

**Note:** No formal mathematical statement currently exists; no active collaborators or formalisation efforts noted.

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 639

Erd≈ës conjectured that in any 2-coloring of the edges of $K_n$, at most $\lfloor n^2/4 \rfloor$
edges do not lie in a monochromatic triangle. This was proved by Keevash and Sudakov for $n \geq 7$.

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/639](https://www.erdosproblems.com/639)

[Er97d] Erd≈ës, P., _Some of my favourite problems in various branches of combinatorics_,
Matematiche (Catania), 1997.

[KeSu04] Keevash, P. and Sudakov, B., _On the number of edges not covered by monochromatic
copies of a fixed graph_, 2004.
-/

namespace Erdos639

/-- A 2-coloring of the edges of the complete graph on $\operatorname{Fin} n$.
Represented as a symmetric function assigning a color ($\operatorname{Bool}$) to each pair
of distinct vertices. -/
structure EdgeTwoColoring (n : ‚Ñï) where
  color : Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Bool
  symm : ‚àÄ u v : Fin n, color u v = color v u

/-- An edge $\{u, v\}$ lies in a monochromatic triangle under coloring $c$ if there
exists a vertex $w$ distinct from both $u$ and $v$ such that all three edges
$\{u,v\}$, $\{u,w\}$, $\{v,w\}$ have the same color. -/
def EdgeTwoColoring.inMonoTriangle {n : ‚Ñï} (c : EdgeTwoColoring n)
    (u v : Fin n) : Prop :=
  ‚àÉ w : Fin n, w ‚âÝ u ‚àß w ‚âÝ v ‚àß
    c.color u v = c.color u w ‚àß c.color u v = c.color v w

/-- The number of edges of $K_n$ (counted as unordered pairs with $u < v$) that
do not lie in any monochromatic triangle under coloring $c$. -/
noncomputable def edgesNotInMonoTriangleCount {n : ‚Ñï} (c : EdgeTwoColoring n) : ‚Ñï :=
  Set.ncard {p : Fin n √ó Fin n | p.1 < p.2 ‚àß ¬¨c.inMonoTriangle p.1 p.2}

/--
Erd≈ës Problem 639 [Er97d]:
If the edges of $K_n$ are 2-coloured then there are at most $\lfloor n^2/4 \rfloor$ edges
which do not occur in a monochromatic triangle.

Solved by Keevash and Sudakov [KeSu04], who proved the threshold is exactly
$\lfloor n^2/4 \rfloor$ for all $n \geq 7$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_639 (n : ‚Ñï) (hn : 7 ‚â§ n) (c : EdgeTwoColoring n) :
    edgesNotInMonoTriangleCount c ‚â§ n ^ 2 / 4 := by
  sorry

end Erdos639

/-

See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/
import Mathlib.Data.Finset.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Fintype.Card
import Mathlib.Data.Nat.Lattice

/-!
# Hypergraph Ramsey Numbers

This file defines the `r`-uniform hypergraph Ramsey number `R_r(n)`.
-/

namespace Combinatorics

/--
The `r`-uniform hypergraph Ramsey number `R_r(n)`.
The smallest natural number `m` such that every 2-coloring of the `r`-subsets of a set of size `m`
contains a monochromatic subset of size `n`.
-/
noncomputable def hypergraphRamsey (r n : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
  sInf { m | ‚àÄ (c : Finset (Fin m) ‚Üí Bool),
    ‚àÉ (S : Finset (Fin m)), S.card = n ‚àß
      ‚àÉ (color : Bool), ‚àÄ (e : Finset (Fin m)), e ‚äÜ S ‚Üí e.card = r ‚Üí c e = color }

/-- `n ‚â§ hypergraphRamsey r n` when the set is nonempty. -/
theorem le_hypergraphRamsey (r n : ‚Ñï) (hne : { m | ‚àÄ (c : Finset (Fin m) ‚Üí Bool),
    ‚àÉ (S : Finset (Fin m)), S.card = n ‚àß
      ‚àÉ (color : Bool), ‚àÄ (e : Finset (Fin m)), e ‚äÜ S ‚Üí e.card = r ‚Üí c e = color }.Nonempty) :
    n ‚â§ hypergraphRamsey r n := by
  apply le_csInf hne
  intro m hm
  have : ‚àÉ (S : Finset (Fin m)), S.card = n ‚àß _ := hm (fun _ ‚Ü¶ false)
  obtain ‚ü®S, hS, -‚ü© := this
  calc n = S.card := hS.symm
    _ ‚â§ Fintype.card (Fin m) := Finset.card_le_univ S
    _ = m := Fintype.card_fin m

/-- `hypergraphRamsey r r = r` for any `r`. -/
theorem hypergraphRamsey_self (r : ‚Ñï) : hypergraphRamsey r r = r := by
  have h_mem : r ‚àà { m | ‚àÄ (c : Finset (Fin m) ‚Üí Bool),
      ‚àÉ (S : Finset (Fin m)), S.card = m ‚àß
        ‚àÉ (color : Bool), ‚àÄ (e : Finset (Fin m)), e ‚äÜ S ‚Üí e.card = r ‚Üí c e = color } := by
    intro c
    refine ‚ü®Finset.univ, ?_, c Finset.univ, ?_‚ü©
    ¬∑ simp [Fintype.card_fin]
    ¬∑ intro e _ he_card
      congr 1
      exact Finset.eq_univ_of_card e (he_card.trans (Fintype.card_fin r).symm)
  exact le_antisymm (Nat.sInf_le h_mem) (le_hypergraphRamsey r r ‚ü®r, h_mem‚ü©)

end Combinatorics

    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 560
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/560](https://www.erdosproblems.com/560)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $\hat{R}(G)$ denote the size Ramsey number, the minimal number of edges $m$ such
    25‚Üíthat there is a graph $H$ with $m$ edges such that in any 2-colouring of the
    26‚Üíedges of $H$ there is a monochromatic copy of $G$.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíDetermine $\hat{R}(K_{n,n})$, where $K_{n,n}$ is the complete bipartite graph with $n$
    29‚Üívertices in each component.
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíKnown bounds:
    32‚Üí$$\frac{1}{60} n^2 \cdot 2^n < \hat{R}(K_{n,n}) < \frac{3}{2} n^3 \cdot 2^n$$
    33‚Üí
    34‚ÜíThe lower bound (for $n \geq 6$) was proved by Erd≈ës and Rousseau [ErRo93].
    35‚ÜíThe upper bound was proved by Erd≈ës, Faudree, Rousseau, and Schelp [EFRS78b]
    36‚Üíand Ne≈°et≈ôil and R√∂dl [NeRo78].
    37‚Üí
    38‚ÜíConlon, Fox, and Wigderson [CFW23] conjecture that $\hat{R}(K_{n,n}) \asymp n^3 \cdot 2^n$.
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí[ErRo93] Erd≈ës, P. and Rousseau, C.C., *The size Ramsey number of a complete bipartite
    41‚Üígraph*, Discrete Mathematics, 1993.
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí[EFRS78b] Erd≈ës, P., Faudree, R.J., Rousseau, C.C., and Schelp, R.H., *The size Ramsey
    44‚Üínumber*, Periodica Mathematica Hungarica, 1978.
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí[NeRo78] Ne≈°et≈ôil, J. and R√∂dl, V., *The Ramsey property for graphs with forbidden complete
    47‚Üísubgraphs*, Journal of Combinatorial Theory, Series B, 1978.
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí[CFW23] Conlon, D., Fox, J., and Wigderson, Y., *Ramsey numbers of books and
    50‚Üíquasirandomness*, Combinatorica, 2023.
    51‚Üí-/
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üínamespace Erdos560
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí/-- The size Ramsey number $\hat{R}(G)$: the minimum number of edges in a graph $H$
    58‚Üíthat is Ramsey for $G$.
    59‚Üí
    60‚ÜíA graph $H$ on $N$ vertices is Ramsey for $G$ if every 2-coloring of the edges
    61‚Üíof $H$ (represented as a symmetric function $c : \operatorname{Fin} N \to \operatorname{Fin} N \to \operatorname{Bool}$)
    62‚Üícontains a monochromatic copy of $G$, i.e., an injective map $f$ from the
    63‚Üívertices of $G$ into $\operatorname{Fin} N$ that preserves adjacency in $H$ and maps all
    64‚Üíedges to the same color. -/
    65‚Üínoncomputable def sizeRamseyNumber {V : Type*} [Fintype V]
    66‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ñï :=
    67‚Üí  sInf {m : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ (N : ‚Ñï) (H : SimpleGraph (Fin N)),
    68‚Üí    Nat.card H.edgeSet = m ‚àß
    69‚Üí    ‚àÄ (c : Fin N ‚Üí Fin N ‚Üí Bool),
    70‚Üí      (‚àÄ i j, c i j = c j i) ‚Üí
    71‚Üí      ‚àÉ (b : Bool) (f : V ‚Üí Fin N),
    72‚Üí        Function.Injective f ‚àß
    73‚Üí        (‚àÄ u v, G.Adj u v ‚Üí H.Adj (f u) (f v)) ‚àß
    74‚Üí        (‚àÄ u v, G.Adj u v ‚Üí c (f u) (f v) = b)}
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí/--
    77‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 560, lower bound [ErRo93]:
    78‚Üí
    79‚ÜíFor all $n \geq 6$, $\hat{R}(K_{n,n}) > \frac{1}{60} n^2 \cdot 2^n$.
    80‚Üí-/
    81‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    82‚Üítheorem erdos_560.variants.lower_bound :
    83‚Üí    ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• 6 ‚Üí
    84‚Üí      (sizeRamseyNumber (completeBipartiteGraph (Fin n) (Fin n)) : ‚Ñù) >
    85‚Üí        (1 / 60 : ‚Ñù) * (n : ‚Ñù) ^ 2 * 2 ^ n := by
    86‚Üí  sorry
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üí/--
    89‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 560, upper bound [EFRS78b, NeRo78]:
    90‚Üí
    91‚ÜíFor all $n \geq 1$, $\hat{R}(K_{n,n}) < \frac{3}{2} n^3 \cdot 2^n$.
    92‚Üí-/
    93‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    94‚Üítheorem erdos_560.variants.upper_bound :
    95‚Üí    ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• 1 ‚Üí
    96‚Üí      (sizeRamseyNumber (completeBipartiteGraph (Fin n) (Fin n)) : ‚Ñù) <
    97‚Üí        (3 / 2 : ‚Ñù) * (n : ‚Ñù) ^ 3 * 2 ^ n := by
    98‚Üí  sorry
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí/--
   101‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 560, conjecture [CFW23]:
   102‚Üí
   103‚Üí$\hat{R}(K_{n,n}) \asymp n^3 \cdot 2^n$, i.e., there exist constants $C_1, C_2 > 0$ and $N_0$
   104‚Üísuch that for all $n \geq N_0$,
   105‚Üí$$C_1 \cdot n^3 \cdot 2^n \leq \hat{R}(K_{n,n}) \leq C_2 \cdot n^3 \cdot 2^n.$$
   106‚Üí-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 560

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/560](https://www.erdosproblems.com/560)

Let $\hat{R}(G)$ denote the size Ramsey number, the minimal number of edges $m$ such
that there is a graph $H$ with $m$ edges such that in any 2-colouring of the
edges of $H$ there is a monochromatic copy of $G$.

Determine $\hat{R}(K_{n,n})$, where $K_{n,n}$ is the complete bipartite graph with $n$
vertices in each component.

Known bounds:
$$\frac{1}{60} n^2 \cdot 2^n < \hat{R}(K_{n,n}) < \frac{3}{2} n^3 \cdot 2^n$$

The lower bound (for $n \geq 6$) was proved by Erd≈ës and Rousseau [ErRo93].
The upper bound was proved by Erd≈ës, Faudree, Rousseau, and Schelp [EFRS78b]
and Ne≈°et≈ôil and R√∂dl [NeRo78].

Conlon, Fox, and Wigderson [CFW23] conjecture that $\hat{R}(K_{n,n}) \asymp n^3 \cdot 2^n$.

[ErRo93] Erd≈ës, P. and Rousseau, C.C., *The size Ramsey number of a complete bipartite
graph*, Discrete Mathematics, 1993.

[EFRS78b] Erd≈ës, P., Faudree, R.J., Rousseau, C.C., and Schelp, R.H., *The size Ramsey
number*, Periodica Mathematica Hungarica, 1978.

[NeRo78] Ne≈°et≈ôil, J. and R√∂dl, V., *The Ramsey property for graphs with forbidden complete
subgraphs*, Journal of Combinatorial Theory, Series B, 1978.

[CFW23] Conlon, D., Fox, J., and Wigderson, Y., *Ramsey numbers of books and
quasirandomness*, Combinatorica, 2023.
-/

open SimpleGraph

namespace Erdos560

/-- The size Ramsey number $\hat{R}(G)$: the minimum number of edges in a graph $H$
that is Ramsey for $G$.

A graph $H$ on $N$ vertices is Ramsey for $G$ if every 2-coloring of the edges
of $H$ (represented as a symmetric function $c : \operatorname{Fin} N \to \operatorname{Fin} N \to \operatorname{Bool}$)
contains a monochromatic copy of $G$, i.e., an injective map $f$ from the
vertices of $G$ into $\operatorname{Fin} N$ that preserves adjacency in $H$ and maps all
edges to the same color. -/
noncomputable def sizeRamseyNumber {V : Type*} [Fintype V]
    (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ñï :=
  sInf {m : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ (N : ‚Ñï) (H : SimpleGraph (Fin N)),
    Nat.card H.edgeSet = m ‚àß
    ‚àÄ (c : Fin N ‚Üí Fin N ‚Üí Bool),
      (‚àÄ i j, c i j = c j i) ‚Üí
      ‚àÉ (b : Bool) (f : V ‚Üí Fin N),
        Function.Injective f ‚àß
        (‚àÄ u v, G.Adj u v ‚Üí H.Adj (f u) (f v)) ‚àß
        (‚àÄ u v, G.Adj u v ‚Üí c (f u) (f v) = b)}

/--
Erd≈ës Problem 560, lower bound [ErRo93]:

For all $n \geq 6$, $\hat{R}(K_{n,n}) > \frac{1}{60} n^2 \cdot 2^n$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_560.variants.lower_bound :
    ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• 6 ‚Üí
      (sizeRamseyNumber (completeBipartiteGraph (Fin n) (Fin n)) : ‚Ñù) >
        (1 / 60 : ‚Ñù) * (n : ‚Ñù) ^ 2 * 2 ^ n := by
  sorry

/--
Erd≈ës Problem 560, upper bound [EFRS78b, NeRo78]:

For all $n \geq 1$, $\hat{R}(K_{n,n}) < \frac{3}{2} n^3 \cdot 2^n$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_560.variants.upper_bound :
    ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• 1 ‚Üí
      (sizeRamseyNumber (completeBipartiteGraph (Fin n) (Fin n)) : ‚Ñù) <
        (3 / 2 : ‚Ñù) * (n : ‚Ñù) ^ 3 * 2 ^ n := by
  sorry

/--
Erd≈ës Problem 560, conjecture [CFW23]:

$\hat{R}(K_{n,n}) \asymp n^3 \cdot 2^n$, i.e., there exist constants $C_1, C_2 > 0$ and $N_0$
such that for all $n \geq N_0$,
$$C_1 \cdot n^3 \cdot 2^n \leq \hat{R}(K_{n,n}) \leq C_2 \cdot n^3 \cdot 2^n.$$
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_560 :
    ‚àÉ C‚ÇÅ : ‚Ñù, C‚ÇÅ > 0 ‚àß ‚àÉ C‚ÇÇ : ‚Ñù, C‚ÇÇ > 0 ‚àß
    ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• N‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
      C‚ÇÅ * (n : ‚Ñù) ^ 3 * 2 ^ n ‚â§
        (sizeRamseyNumber (completeBipartiteGraph (Fin n) (Fin n)) : ‚Ñù) ‚àß
      (sizeRamseyNumber (completeBipartiteGraph (Fin n) (Fin n)) : ‚Ñù) ‚â§
        C‚ÇÇ * (n : ‚Ñù) ^ 3 * 2 ^ n := by
  sorry

end Erdos560

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 161

Erd≈ës conjectured that for $t$-uniform hypergraph 2-colourings, the balanced-colouring threshold
$F^{(t)}(n,\alpha)$ grows polynomially in $\log n$ for any $\alpha > 0$, in contrast with the
iterated-logarithm growth at $\alpha = 0$.

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/161](https://www.erdosproblems.com/161)

[Er90b] Erd≈ës, P., _Problems and results on graphs and hypergraphs: similarities and
differences_. Mathematics of Ramsey theory, Algorithms Combin. 5 (1990), 12-28.

[CFS11] Conlon, D., Fox, J., and Sudakov, B., _An approximate version of Sidorenko's
conjecture_. Geometric and Functional Analysis 20 (2010), 1354-1366.
-/

open Finset Real

namespace Erdos161

/-- A 2-colouring $c$ of the $t$-element subsets of $\text{Fin}(n)$ is $\alpha$-balanced at
threshold $m$ if for every subset $X \subseteq \text{Fin}(n)$ with $|X| \geq m$, each colour class
contains at least $\alpha \binom{|X|}{t}$ many $t$-element subsets of $X$. -/
def IsBalancedColouring (n t m : ‚Ñï) (Œ± : ‚Ñù) (c : Finset (Fin n) ‚Üí Bool) : Prop :=
  ‚àÄ X : Finset (Fin n), m ‚â§ X.card ‚Üí
    ‚àÄ b : Bool,
      Œ± * (Nat.choose X.card t : ‚Ñù) ‚â§
        (((X.powersetCard t).filter (fun S => c S = b)).card : ‚Ñù)

/-- $F^{(t)}(n, \alpha)$ is the smallest $m$ such that there exists a 2-colouring of the
$t$-element subsets of $[n]$ that is $\alpha$-balanced at threshold $m$. -/
noncomputable def fBalanced (t n : ‚Ñï) (Œ± : ‚Ñù) : ‚Ñï :=
  sInf { m : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ c : Finset (Fin n) ‚Üí Bool, IsBalancedColouring n t m Œ± c }

/--
Erd≈ës Conjecture (Problem 161) [Er90b, p.21]:

For any $t \geq 2$ and $0 < \alpha < 1/2$, the function $F^{(t)}(n, \alpha)$ is bounded by a
polynomial in $\log n$. Specifically, there exist constants $C > 0$ and $D > 0$
(depending on $t$ and $\alpha$) such that $F^{(t)}(n, \alpha) \leq C \cdot (\log n)^D$ for all
sufficiently large $n$.

This captures Erd≈ës's conjecture that "the jump occurs all in one step at $0$":
for $\alpha > 0$, $F^{(t)}(n, \alpha)$ grows polynomially in $\log n$, contrasting with the
much slower iterated-logarithm growth $F^{(t)}(n, 0) \approx \log_{t-1}(n)$.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_161 :
    ‚àÄ t : ‚Ñï, 2 ‚â§ t ‚Üí
    ‚àÄ Œ± : ‚Ñù, 0 < Œ± ‚Üí Œ± < 1 / 2 ‚Üí
    ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, 0 < C ‚àß
    ‚àÉ D : ‚Ñù, 0 < D ‚àß
    ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, N‚ÇÄ ‚â§ n ‚Üí
      (fBalanced t n Œ± : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ C * (Real.log (n : ‚Ñù)) ^ D := by
  sorry

end Erdos161

# Review: Erd≈ës Problem 563

## 1. Code Reuse

**Several opportunities identified:**

- **`EdgeTwoColoring` structure (Problems 639, 76):** Problem 639 defines a reusable `EdgeTwoColoring` structure bundling a `Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Bool` colouring with a symmetry proof. Problem 563 currently inlines the symmetry condition `‚àÄ i j, c i j = c j i` inside `fErdos563`. Adopting the `EdgeTwoColoring` structure (or a shared version) would improve consistency across problems 559, 560, 561, 563, and 639.

- **Problem 161 (`fBalanced`, `IsBalancedColouring`):** Problem 161 is the direct hypergraph generalization of Problem 563. Its `fBalanced` and `IsBalancedColouring` definitions handle `t`-uniform hypergraphs. Problem 563 is the special case `t = 2`. In principle, `fErdos563 n Œ±` should be equivalent to `Erdos161.fBalanced 2 n Œ±` (up to strict vs. non-strict inequality ‚Äî see Correctness below). However, Problem 161 colours `t`-element *subsets* (as `Finset (Fin n) ‚Üí Bool`) rather than using a symmetric pair function, so the representations differ. Unifying these would require a bridging lemma, which may not be worth the complexity ‚Äî but the relationship should at least be documented.

- **`edgesOfColorInSubset`:** This function is unique to Problem 563. No other problem defines an identical edge-counting helper, though Problem 639's `edgesNotInMonoTriangleCount` uses a similar `p.1 < p.2` filtering pattern. The implementation is reasonable and self-contained.

- **`hypergraphRamsey` (FormalConjecturesForMathlib):** The Ramsey number definition is conceptually related (Œ± = 0 case) but structurally different enough that direct reuse is impractical.

**Verdict:** Minor reuse opportunities exist (shared `EdgeTwoColoring` type), but the current self-contained approach is acceptable given the problem's specific quantitative structure.

## 2. Citations

**Website ([erdosproblems.com/563](https://www.erdosproblems.com/563)) states:**
> [Er90b] Erd≈ës, P., *Problems and results on graphs and hypergraphs: similarities and differences*. Mathematics of Ramsey theory, Algorithms Combin. 5 (1990), 12-28.

**Formalization states:**
> [Er90b] Erd≈ës, P., *Problems and results on graphs and hypergraphs: similarities and differences* (1990), p.21.

**Assessment:** The citation is essentially correct. The formalization includes the page number (p.21), which matches the website's listing as originating from that page. The full journal information ("Mathematics of Ramsey theory, Algorithms Combin. 5, 12-28") is omitted in the formalization but the shorthand is adequate and consistent with other problems in the codebase. The website also notes this is listed as "#39 in Ramsey Theory" in the UCSD graph problem collection, which the formalization does not mention ‚Äî this is fine to omit.

**No missing citations.** The website lists no additional references beyond [Er90b].

## 3. Variants

**Website mentions:**
1. The case Œ± = 0 reduces to classical Ramsey numbers (asking for 2-colourings with no monochromatic clique of size m).
2. The probabilistic method gives F(n, Œ±) ‚âç_Œ± log n (matching asymptotic order but not the precise constant).
3. Problem 161 generalizes this to t-uniform hypergraphs.

**Formalization captures:** Only the main conjecture (F(n, Œ±) ~ c_Œ± ¬∑ log n).

**Assessment:** The formalization correctly restricts to the core conjecture. The Œ± = 0 / Ramsey connection is a remark rather than a variant. The F(n, Œ±) ‚âç log n result (known via the probabilistic method) could be stated as a separate solved theorem, since it is a known partial result. Problem 161 already exists as a separate formalization. No critical variants are missing.

**Potential addition:** A solved variant stating `‚àÉ C‚ÇÅ C‚ÇÇ > 0, ‚àÄ·∂Ý n, C‚ÇÅ * log n ‚â§ F(n,Œ±) ‚â§ C‚ÇÇ * log n` would capture the known asymptotic order.

## 4. Readability

**Strengths:**
- The module docstring clearly explains the problem with LaTeX notation.
- The helper `edgesOfColorInSubset` is well-documented and cleanly separates edge-counting from the main definition.
- The `fErdos563` docstring explains the mathematical meaning.

**Suggestions:**
- The `edgesOfColorInSubset` docstring mentions "An edge {i, j} (with i < j) has colour c(i,j)" but the function does not require or enforce that `c` is symmetric. This is fine for the implementation (the caller enforces symmetry), but could be clarified.
- The filter condition `fun p => p.1 < p.2 ‚àß c p.1 p.2 = color` is compact and correct. No issues.
- `open Finset Filter` and `open scoped Topology` are appropriate for the definitions and theorem used.

**Overall readability: Good.** The code is concise and well-structured.

## 5. Formalizability

**Assessment: High formalizability with one subtlety.**

The problem statement on the website is:
> F(n, Œ±) ~ c_Œ± ¬∑ log n

The asymptotic notation "~" means F(n,Œ±) / log(n) ‚Üí c_Œ± as n ‚Üí ‚àû. This is precise and unambiguous once F(n, Œ±) is defined.

The definition of F(n, Œ±) itself ‚Äî "the smallest m such that there exists a 2-colouring of K_n such that every m-subset contains more than Œ± ¬∑ C(m,2) edges of each colour" ‚Äî is precise enough to formalize, though there is a subtlety about whether the threshold applies to subsets of size *exactly* m or *at least* m. The formalization uses "‚â• m", which is the standard and correct interpretation (once the property holds for all sets of size ‚â• m, it defines the threshold).

**Ambiguity level: Low.** The only potential source of ambiguity is the strict inequality "> Œ± ¬∑ C(m,2)" vs. non-strict "‚â• Œ± ¬∑ C(m,2)". The website uses "more than", which maps to strict inequality. The formalization correctly uses strict inequality (`>`). Note that Problem 161 uses non-strict inequality (`‚â§` on the other side, i.e., `Œ± * ... ‚â§ count`), which is equivalent to `count ‚â• Œ± * ...`, a non-strict version. This is a minor inconsistency between the two related problems ‚Äî see Correctness.

## 6. Correctness

**Definition of `edgesOfColorInSubset`:** Correct. It counts pairs `(i, j)` with `i < j` in `X √óÀ¢ X` where `c i j = color`. The `i < j` condition ensures each edge is counted exactly once. This equals the number of edges of the given colour in the induced subgraph on X.

**Definition of `fErdos563`:** This uses `sInf` over the set of valid `m` values. The structure is:
```
sInf {m : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ c (symmetric), ‚àÄ X with |X| ‚â• m, ‚àÄ color, edgeCount > Œ± ¬∑ C(|X|,2)}
```

**Potential issue ‚Äî direction of the quantifier over colourings:**

The website states: *"the smallest m such that there exists a 2-colouring of K_n where every subset X with |X| ‚â• m contains more than Œ±¬∑C(|X|,2) edges of each colour."*

The formalization uses `‚àÉ c, ... ‚àÄ X ...`, meaning: there *exists* a colouring such that every large subset is balanced. This matches the website. The idea is that F(n, Œ±) measures how large subsets need to be before *some* colouring can guarantee balance everywhere ‚Äî this is the correct "minimax" reading where we minimize over m and maximize (existentially) over colourings.

**However**, this interpretation is somewhat unusual. Typically in Ramsey theory, one asks about colourings adversarially (‚àÄ colourings). Let me re-read the website carefully:

> "Let F(n,Œ±) denote the smallest m such that there exists a 2-colouring of edges of K_n where every subset X ‚äÜ [n] with |X| ‚â• m contains more than Œ±¬∑(|X| choose 2) edges of each colour."

The formalization matches this literally: F(n,Œ±) = inf{m | ‚àÉ colouring c, ‚àÄ X with |X|‚â•m, both colours are well-represented}. This is correct. The interpretation is: a "good" colouring is one that is so balanced that even in moderate subsets both colours appear substantially. F(n,Œ±) asks: how large must subsets be before such a good colouring exists?

**Strict vs. non-strict inequality:** The formalization uses `>` (strict), matching "more than" on the website. Problem 161 uses `‚â§` (i.e., `Œ± * C(|X|,t) ‚â§ count`, non-strict). For the asymptotic conjecture, this distinction is immaterial (it can only affect F by ¬±1 at most), but it is a formal inconsistency between the two related problems.

**`C(m,2)` computation:** The formalization computes `C(|X|,2)` as `|X| * (|X| - 1) / 2` using real arithmetic. This is correct: for natural number `k`, `C(k,2) = k(k-1)/2`. The use of real-valued expressions avoids natural number subtraction issues.

**Theorem statement:** `Tendsto (fun n => F(n,Œ±) / log n) atTop (nhds c)` for some `c > 0`. This correctly captures `F(n,Œ±) ~ c_Œ± ¬∑ log n`.

**Edge case:** When n = 0 or n = 1, `fErdos563` returns `sInf ‚àÖ = 0` if no valid m exists, or `sInf` of some set. The theorem statement uses `atTop` so only the asymptotic behaviour matters, making edge cases irrelevant.

**Verdict: Mathematically correct.** The formalization faithfully captures the conjecture as stated on the website. The only minor issue is the strict-vs-non-strict inconsistency with Problem 161, which does not affect correctness of this problem in isolation.

---

## Summary

| Criterion | Rating | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Code Reuse | Minor opportunities | Could share `EdgeTwoColoring`; relationship to Problem 161 could be documented |

**Definition of `edgesOfColorInSubset`:** Correct. It counts pairs `(i, j)` with `i < j` in `X √óÀ¢ X` where `c i j = color`. The `i < j` condition ensures each edge is counted exactly once. This equals the number of edges of the given colour in the induced subgraph on X.

**Definition of `fErdos563`:** This uses `sInf` over the set of valid `m` values. The structure is:

sInf {m : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ c (symmetric), ‚àÄ X with |X| ‚â• m, ‚àÄ color, edgeCount > Œ± ¬∑ C(|X|,2)}

The website states: *"the smallest m such that there exists a 2-colouring of K_n where every subset X with |X| ‚â• m contains more than Œ±¬∑C(|X|,2) edges of each colour."*

The formalization uses `‚àÉ c, ... ‚àÄ X ...`, meaning: there *exists* a colouring such that every large subset is balanced. This matches the website. The idea is that F(n, Œ±) measures how large subsets need to be before *some* colouring can guarantee balance everywhere ‚Äî this is the correct "minimax" reading where we minimize over m and maximize (existentially) over colourings.

**However**, this interpretation is somewhat unusual. Typically in Ramsey theory, one asks about colourings adversarially (‚àÄ colourings). Let me re-read the website carefully:

> "Let F(n,Œ±) denote the smallest m such that there exists a 2-colouring of edges of K_n where every subset X ‚äÜ [n] with |X| ‚â• m contains more than Œ±¬∑(|X| choose 2) edges of each colour."

The interpretation is: a "good" colouring is one that is so balanced that even in moderate subsets both colours appear substantially. F(n,Œ±) asks: how large must subsets be before such a good colouring exists?
